comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: FORCE_CLAIM: plan-review gate bypassed by user approval — no plan-review attestation was verified. Reason: "landed on main via Gerrit (#143/#144/#163/#164, merged); plan-review is moot for already-merged code — closing to run the completion-verifier gate".

compiled_state.description:
## Why

The floor must key on VERIFIED delivery, never bare `closed` status — otherwise a force-closed or
abandoned child would launder un-validated work past scrutiny, and a stale closed ticket can hard-
block the epic review (observed in practice).

## What

Add a `delivered_now(child, epic, repo_root)` helper (in `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py`,
reusing the `compute_validity(sig, child_state, 'completion-verifier')` validity-on-read check used INSIDE `_child_closure_findings` in `src/rebar/llm/completion.py` — note that fn is a DEFICIT detector (returns findings for children LACKING delivery), so we reuse its validity CALL applied to children whose status IS closed, NOT the fn itself) that returns True iff
the child is `closed` AND holds a completion-verifier attestation that is VALID on read
(`compute_validity(..., "completion-verifier")` — so drift-stale / force-closed / unsigned all read
false), OR child B is superseded by a SIBLING A (same parent; direction B superseded_by A) that is itself a live vehicle (delivered-now OR open/in_progress, not force-closed/abandoned) (read the
`supersedes`/`duplicates` links via `src/rebar/__init__.py`; verify shared parent so work that left
the epic does not count as delivered). Recomputed each run. In-progress / abandoned / force-closed
children return False -> stay in scope.

## Scope

- IN: the `delivered_now` predicate + its sibling-supersede check + validity-on-read reuse.
- OUT: the Pass-3 wiring (separate story); the Pass-2 sub-call (separate story).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `delivered_now` is True for a closed child with a VALID completion attestation; False for force-closed, unsigned, drift-stale, in-progress, and abandoned children.
- [ ] `delivered_now` treats B as delivered-elsewhere ONLY when a SIBLING A (same parent) supersedes B (direction: B superseded_by A) AND A is a live in-epic vehicle (delivered-now OR active open/in_progress); False when A is not a sibling or is itself force-closed/abandoned (closes the supersede-direction + replacement-not-done gap).
- [ ] The predicate reuses the `compute_validity(..., 'completion-verifier')` validity-on-read call (NOT the deficit-detector `_child_closure_findings` itself), applied to closed children, and is recomputed per run (no persisted state).
- [ ] Unit tests cover attested / force-closed / drift-stale / supersede-sibling / supersede-nonsibling / in-progress cases.

## Supersede mechanism (concrete artifact)

The supersede relationship is the structured `supersedes` LINK RELATION (one of rebar's six link
relations) — an auditable, event-sourced graph edge in the ticket's deps, NOT a free-text note,
tag, or naming convention. `delivered_now` reads it via `rebar.show`/`ticket_deps`: child B is
delivered-elsewhere iff a SIBLING A (same `parent_id`) has an `A supersedes B` edge (equivalently B
`superseded_by` A) AND A is a live in-epic vehicle (delivered-now OR open/in_progress). Because the
edge is a structured graph relation (not unsigned prose) AND the superseding sibling A stays in
review scope, this is NOT the unsigned/gameable suppression the Alternatives reject: no coverage is
removed (A is still reviewed), and the security/contract preserve-set (T5c/T10) is never dropped
regardless of supersede — so a manipulated edge cannot launder a security finding.

## Acceptance Criteria (mechanism)

- [ ] The supersede signal is the structured `supersedes` link relation read from the child's deps (named artifact), not a note/tag/convention.
- [ ] A test asserts the security/contract preserve-set is never dropped even when a supersede edge is present (no laundering).

## Acceptance Criteria (reopen semantics)

- [ ] A PARENT epic reopen does NOT un-deliver its closed children: ADR 0009 keys on each ticket's OWN `last_reopened_at`, so only a CHILD's own reopen invalidates that child's completion attestation. Closed children remain `delivered-now` across a parent reopen; a reopened child flips to not-delivered and its findings resurface.

file_impact.reason: read supersedes/duplicates links + parent for the sibling-supersede check

compiled_state.title: delivered-now predicate: reuse completion attestation (validity-on-read) + in-epic sibling supersede

verify_commands.dd_text: delivered_now true only for attested-closed or in-epic-supersede
force-closed / drift-stale / non-sibling-supersede all read not-delivered
